page: Amazon Web Services d3en.8xlarge is a dense storage instance optimized for network and EBS performance. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs operating at 3.1 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A key feature of this instance is its massive local storage capacity, offering 223,680 GB of HDD storage, coupled with a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the d3en.8xlarge delivers average multi-core CPU capabilities and memory bandwidth. While single-core performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 25%, its multi-threaded execution, including multi-core Geekbench and bzip3 decompression, achieves average tier results. Memory read-only bandwidth reaches up to 1551.24 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 109.98 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server provides high resource density specifically for storage-heavy workloads. The combination of massive HDD capacity and 50 Gbps network throughput makes it highly suitable for data-intensive applications. However, the trade-off lies in its lower single-core compute performance and lack of GPU acceleration. This instance is best suited for large-scale data warehousing, distributed file systems, log processing, and high-capacity storage databases where sequential throughput and storage volume are prioritized over raw single-threaded compute speed.
description: Amazon Web Services d3en.8xlarge is a dense storage instance designed for high-capacity workloads requiring substantial local storage and network throughput. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor, it features 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance stands out for its massive 223,680 GB of local HDD storage and a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, though single-core processing speeds are relatively weak. This hardware profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for storage-dense configurations, making it a strong fit for distributed file systems, data warehousing, and large-scale log processing. However, the lack of GPU acceleration and lower single-threaded performance make it less suitable for compute-heavy or real-time rendering tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d3en.8xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, 223,680 GB of local HDD storage, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services d3en.8xlarge features 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 223,680 GB HDD storage, optimized for high-capacity storage and network workloads.
tagline: A dense storage instance featuring massive local HDD capacity and high network bandwidth for data-intensive and distributed file system workloads.

128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz
Massive 223,680 GB of local HDD storage capacity
